text.title: Computer Use Tool Setup and Safety
text.summary: Rules for configuring the Computer Use tool, implementing action handlers, and enforcing safety requirements for sandboxed execution.

rule.statement: Always set exact display dimensions (display_width_px, display_height_px) matching the actual VM resolution.
rule.rationale: The model uses pixel coordinates for click and mouse_move actions. Mismatched dimensions produce clicks at wrong positions.

rule.statement: Use the versioned tool type string (e.g., "computer_20241022") and include the matching beta header.
rule.rationale: Computer Use tool schemas are versioned. Mixing tool type version with a different beta header raises an API error.

rule.statement: Implement handlers for all actions: screenshot, mouse_move, left_click, right_click, type, key.
rule.rationale: The model may call any action in the schema. An unhandled action raises a runtime error that breaks the session.

rule.statement: Always capture a screenshot after each action cycle and return it as the tool result.
rule.rationale: The model uses the updated screenshot to decide the next action. Without a current screenshot, it reasons over stale state.

rule.statement: Implement a per-action timeout and a maximum iteration count for each session.
rule.rationale: Computer Use can loop indefinitely on unexpected UI states. Timeouts and iteration limits prevent runaway sessions.

rule.statement: Run Computer Use in an isolated VM or container with limited permissions — no sudo, no live credentials.
rule.rationale: The model generates OS-level commands. Executing them on a host machine without isolation is a critical security risk.

rule.statement: Route unexpected UI states to human review rather than letting the agent retry autonomously.
rule.rationale: Agents encountering unfamiliar UI can make destructive actions. Human escalation prevents data loss or unintended system modifications.

example.code:
computer_tool = { "type": "computer_20241022", "name": "computer", "display_width_px": 1920, "display_height_px": 1080, "display_number": 1 }
message = client.beta.messages.create( model="claude-sonnet-4-20250514", max_tokens=4096, tools=[computer_tool], betas=["computer-use-2024-10-22"], messages=[{"role": "user", "content": "Open Chrome and go to example.com"}] )
example.note: Tool type version and beta header must match exactly.

antipattern.description: Running Computer Use directly on the host machine without sandboxing.
antipattern.reason: The model generates click, type, and key commands that execute against whatever is on screen. On a production host this can delete files, submit forms, or leak credentials.
